title: Steph Tolev at Helium Comedy Club
description: Sharp, unapologetic standup from Steph Tolev (Netflix 'Filth Queen'). Midweek date night done right - dinner in Midtown first, then split laughs. Cozy club with table service keeps the date-night vibe dialed up.

title: A FINE LINE - Four-Artist Show at Spalding Nix Fine Art
location: Spalding Nix Fine Art, Atlanta, GA
description: Four artists (Kate Hooray Osmond, Nancy Talero, Shannon Davis, Laura Dargan) come together for this intimate group show. Free, conversation-friendly, and a great excuse to slow down together midweek.

title: Tropical Latin Night Atlanta
description: Friday-night kickoff with tropical Latin rhythms, reggaeton, and high-energy dancing. Couples who love a club scene will thrive here - dress up, grab a cocktail, and dance until 2am.

title: Like a Star: Celebrating 20 Years of Corinne Bailey Rae
description: Soulful, romantic R&B from Corinne Bailey Rae celebrating 20 years of her debut. Warm vocals, lush arrangements, and one of the most date-night-coded venues in the city. Splurge on balcony seats if you can.

title: Friday Night Traffic Jam & Jokes: Young Gunnas of Comedy
description: Rising Black comedy talent bringing fresh perspectives. Off-the-beaten-path Forest Park venue makes it feel like an inside-baseball date for couples who love supporting the next wave of comedians.

title: Blooms Market at Ponce
location: The Shed at Ponce City Market, Atlanta, GA
description: Monthly BeltLine-adjacent market with local artists, makers, and food. Wander hand-in-hand, browse, then grab a drink on the PCM roof. A relaxed Saturday morning date that turns into a full afternoon.

title: King of Pops Yoga on the BeltLine
location: Historic Fourth Ward Park - Greenspace D, Atlanta, GA
description: Free outdoor yoga on the BeltLine, then popsicles from King of Pops. Active couples who want a low-pressure morning date - move together, then reward yourselves. Pair with a BeltLine brunch crawl.

title: Tropical Elegance Saturday Latin Night Atlanta
description: Saturday salsa + bachata lesson, then open dance floor. Beginner-friendly, no partner required, and the suburban Alpharetta location is a change of pace from the usual ATL clubs. Worth the drive for couples who want a fuller night.

title: Art of the Game Opening Reception
location: Gallery Chimera, 624 N Highland Ave NE, Atlanta
description: World Cup-inspired group exhibition featuring international artists exploring soccer, culture, and unity. Opening reception = wine, artists, and great conversation. Runs June 11 - July 11 if you can't make opening night.

title: Love and Music - Atlanta! Romantic Piano Concert
time: 7:30 PM - 9:30 PM (VIP cocktail hour 7:30 PM, concert 8:30 PM)
description: VIP cocktail hour followed by a romantic piano concert immersion. Literally built for couples - this is the most date-night-coded event of the week. Splurge-worthy for an anniversary or special occasion.

title: Love Lust Language Live Music & Poetry Show
description: Intimate music + poetry night exploring love and desire. Late-night vibes, raw performances, and the kind of show that sparks real conversation on the walk home. Bring your partner who loves words.

title: La Placita Latin Sundays ATL - Dunwoody
location: Spruill Center for the Arts, Dunwoody, GA
description: Recurring Sunday Latin social with dancing, food, and community. Family-friendly vibe makes it a great first-date option - low pressure, lively music, no cover. Free to attend!

title: Salsa Bachata Havana Nights
description: Atlanta's premier Sunday Latin party - lessons early, then open dance floor all night. Beginner-friendly, no partner required, and the energy is unmatched. Couples who take a class together stay together.

title: STAMPED: AYA x Friends - Amapiano & Afrobeats
description: One of Atlanta's hottest Amapiano and Afrobeats parties. Afrobeats lovers, this is the move. Outdoor-adjacent venue, food, and a young crowd ready to dance. Sunday Funday at its finest.

title: Pyramid Plant Based Veg Fest
description: Veg-forward festival with vendors, food, and plant-based living demos. Worth the drive to Cumming for any plant-curious couple. Browse, taste, and stock up on new staples together.

title: Waiting to Exhale Dinner Series Volume 1
description: Curated multi-course dinner experience. Perfect for couples who appreciate intentional dining, conversation, and a step above your usual restaurant night. Reserve early - these intimate series fill fast.

title: Delvon Lamarr Organ Trio at Terminal West
location: Terminal West, West Midtown, Atlanta, GA
description: Soul-soaked jazz-funk trio serving up B-3 grooves all night. Intimate West Midtown venue, perfect sound, easy to grab a cocktail beforehand. A 'wow, how did we not know about this band?' date night.

title: Spyro Gyra Live at City Winery
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Legendary jazz fusion group at one of Atlanta's most romantic venues. Wine, dinner, and a band with 40+ years of catalog. Couples who dig sophisticated music - this is the move. Reserve a table for 2.

title: Rhys Darby at Helium Comedy Club
description: The Flight of the Conchords star brings his surreal, story-driven comedy. Quirky, smart, and surprisingly romantic-adjacent - perfect for the couple whose humor runs more 'absurd' than 'blue'.

title: Juneteenth Atlanta Parade and Music Festival
time: All Day (parade 10 AM, festival all day)
description: 14th Annual celebration - one of the Southeast's biggest cultural events. Parade, multiple music stages, food vendors (veg-friendly options!), and community energy. Free, outdoors, and a true Atlanta experience.

title: ROOTS + REMIX: Art, Design, Identity & Sound
description: Multidisciplinary night blending art, design, and live sound. Creative couples who like their dates concept-driven will love this. Hapeville is the under-the-radar part of the night - get there early.

title: Tropical Latin Night Atlanta
description: Juneteenth-weekend edition of ATL's most reliable Latin party. Reggaeton, salsa, bachata, and a packed dance floor. Couples who want to celebrate the holiday weekend with movement - this is the move.

title: Happy People Atlanta
description: Culture-meets-party: 8 hours of music, food, and pure Juneteenth-weekend vibes in Underground Atlanta. Couples who like their dates long, lively, and rooted in Black culture will love this. Arrive early, stay late.

title: Soul, Jazz & Funk Pre-Father's Day Concert
location: Mable House Barnes Amphitheatre, Mableton, GA
description: Open-air amphitheater, picnic-style seating, and a soul-jazz-funk lineup. Bring a blanket, wine, and your favorite person. One of the most romantic outdoor concert setups in metro Atlanta.

title: Reggae on the Rooftop Festival - Juneteenth Weekend
location: AIR ROOFROP (formerly Suite Lounge), Atlanta, GA
description: Six hours of reggae on a rooftop, paired with food and Juneteenth weekend energy. Couples who love outdoor festivals, island vibes, and dancing - this is the weekend's top pick.

title: Atlanta BeltLine Fest at Pittsburgh Yards
location: Pittsburgh Yards, 352 University Ave SW, Atlanta, GA
description: Free two-day festival on the new Southside Trail - live World Cup match screenings, music, DJs, food vendors, art. Spend the day outside together without spending a dime. Bring a blanket and a sense of adventure.

title: Sip & Savor: Wine & Charcuterie Tasting
location: Koncept House Capitol View, Atlanta, GA
description: Guided wine + charcuterie pairing experience. Perfect for the foodie couple who wants something more interactive than a regular dinner. Great icebreaker energy and you leave with new wine picks.

title: Vegan Soul Sunday - Father's Day Edition
location: New Life Presbyterian Church, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based community cookout with vendors, soul-food classics, and good vibes. Free entry makes it a no-brainer Sunday outing for veg-curious couples or anyone who wants to support local food makers.

title: Salsa at Politan Row at Colony Square
location: Politan Row, Colony Square, Midtown Atlanta
description: Sunday afternoon salsa + bachata social surrounded by food hall options. Free to dance, easy to grab a drink or bite, and the energy is welcoming for newbies. Date-night gold in the middle of Midtown.

title: Plant to the Future: Food, Freedom & Black Futures Conference
location: Pittsburgh Yards - The Food Studio, Atlanta, GA
description: Day-long conference on plant-based food, Black futures, and food justice. Panels, vendors, cooking demos. For the veg-curious couple who also likes a brainy, values-driven day together.
